(Spanish pronunciation: [[la ˈplata]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")) is the capital city of [Buenos Aires province](/wiki/Buenos_Aires_province "Buenos Aires province"), Argentina. According to the 2022 [census](/wiki/Argentina_census "Argentina census"), the [Partido](/wiki/La_Plata_Partido "La Plata Partido") has a population of 772,618 and its metropolitan area, the [Greater La Plata](/wiki/Greater_La_Plata "Greater La Plata"), has 938,287 inhabitants.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It is located 9 kilometers (6 miles) inland from the southern shore of the [Río de la Plata](/wiki/R%C3%ADo_de_la_Plata "Río de la Plata") estuary.

**La Plata** (Spanish pronunciation: [[la ˈplata]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")) is the capital city of [Buenos Aires province](/wiki/Buenos_Aires_province "Buenos Aires province"), Argentina. According to the 2022 [census](/wiki/Argentina_census "Argentina census"), the [Partido](/wiki/La_Plata_Partido "La Plata Partido") has a population of 772,618 and its metropolitan area, the [Greater La Plata](/wiki/Greater_La_Plata "Greater La Plata"), has 938,287 inhabitants.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It is located 9 kilometers (6 miles) inland from the southern shore of the [Río de la Plata](/wiki/R%C3%ADo_de_la_Plata "Río de la Plata") estuary.

La Plata was planned and developed to serve as the provincial capital after the city of [Buenos Aires](/wiki/Buenos_Aires "Buenos Aires") was [federalized](/wiki/Federalization_of_Buenos_Aires "Federalization of Buenos Aires") in 1880. It was officially founded by Governor [Dardo Rocha](/wiki/Dardo_Rocha "Dardo Rocha") on 19 November 1882. Its construction is fully documented in photographs by Tomás Bradley Sutton.[[3]](#cite_note-3) La Plata was briefly known as *Ciudad Eva Perón* ([Eva Perón](/wiki/Eva_Per%C3%B3n "Eva Perón") City) between 1952 and 1955.

## History and description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History and description")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/DardoRocha.jpg/250px-DardoRocha.jpg)](/wiki/File:DardoRocha.jpg)

Governor [Dardo Rocha](/wiki/Dardo_Rocha "Dardo Rocha") (1838–1921), founder of La Plata.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/be/Crop_of_ISS067-E-176677_La_Plata%2C_Argentina_and_vicinity.jpg/250px-Crop_of_ISS067-E-176677_La_Plata%2C_Argentina_and_vicinity.jpg)](/wiki/File:Crop_of_ISS067-E-176677_La_Plata,_Argentina_and_vicinity.jpg)

The city and vicinity at 8:19 pm locally on 4 July 2022, taken from the International Space Station.

After La Plata was designated the provincial capital, Rocha was placed in charge of creating the city. He hired urban planner [Pedro Benoit](/wiki/Pedro_Benoit "Pedro Benoit"), who designed a city layout based on a [rationalist](/wiki/Rationalism "Rationalism") [conception of urban centers](/wiki/Urban_planning "Urban planning"). The city has the shape of a square with a central park and two main diagonal avenues, north to south and east to west. In addition, there are numerous other shorter diagonal streets. This design is copied in a [self-similar manner](/wiki/Fractals "Fractals") in small blocks of six by six blocks in length. For every six blocks, there is a small park or square. Other than the diagonal streets, all streets are on a rectangular grid and are numbered consecutively. Thus, La Plata is nicknamed "la ciudad de las diagonales" (city of diagonals). It is also called "la ciudad de los tilos" (city of [linden trees](/wiki/Linden_tree "Linden tree")), because of the large number of linden trees lining the many streets and squares. The linden tree is one of a number of deciduous Northern Hemisphere tree species which dominate La Plata's parks and streets; [ash](/wiki/Fraxinus "Fraxinus"), [horsechestnut](/wiki/Aesculus_hippocastanum "Aesculus hippocastanum"), [plane](/wiki/Platanus "Platanus"), [sweetgum](/wiki/Liquidambar_styraciflua "Liquidambar styraciflua") and [tulip tree](/wiki/Liriodendron_tulipifera "Liriodendron tulipifera") are among the other examples. [Palms](/wiki/Palm_(plant) "Palm (plant)") and subtropical broadleaf evergreen trees thrive but are comparatively infrequent.

The city design and its buildings are noted to possess strong [Freemason](/wiki/Freemason "Freemason") symbolism as a consequence of both Rocha and Benoit being Freemasons.[[4]](#cite_note-TMT-4)

The designs for the government buildings were chosen in an international architectural competition. Thus, the Governor Palace was designed by Italians, the City Hall by Germans, etc. Electric street lighting was installed in 1884 and was the first of its kind in Latin America.

### Important landmarks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Important landmarks")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/03/Buenos_Aires_-_La_Plata1.jpg/250px-Buenos_Aires_-_La_Plata1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Buenos_Aires_-_La_Plata1.jpg)

[Cathedral of La Plata](/wiki/Cathedral_of_La_Plata "Cathedral of La Plata")

The neo-Gothic [cathedral of La Plata](/wiki/Cathedral_of_La_Plata "Cathedral of La Plata") is the largest church in Argentina. It is located on the central park, Plaza Moreno, and is the 58th tallest church in the world.

The [Teatro Argentino de La Plata](/wiki/Teatro_Argentino_de_La_Plata "Teatro Argentino de La Plata") is one of the most important [opera houses](/wiki/Opera_house "Opera house") in Argentina, second to the [Teatro Colón](/wiki/Teatro_Col%C3%B3n "Teatro Colón") in Buenos Aires. The construction was funded by the first inhabitants of La Plata, but as maintenance was very expensive it was later donated to the Province of Buenos Aires. On 18 October 1977, the building was almost completely destroyed by a fire. This has been noted as one of the largest losses to La Plata's historical heritage. It was later replaced by a new building, which houses the theatre's orchestra, choir and ballet, boasting several halls.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ad/PA280461.JPG/250px-PA280461.JPG)](/wiki/File:PA280461.JPG)

Panoramic view of La Plata.

The [Curutchet House](/wiki/Curutchet_House "Curutchet House") is one of the two buildings by [Le Corbusier](/wiki/Le_Corbusier "Le Corbusier") built in the Americas.

The [University of La Plata](/wiki/University_of_La_Plata "University of La Plata") was founded in 1897 and nationalized in 1905. It is well known for its [observatory](/wiki/La_Plata_Astronomical_Observatory "La Plata Astronomical Observatory") and [natural history museum](/wiki/La_Plata_Museum "La Plata Museum"). [Ernesto Sabato](/wiki/Ernesto_Sabato "Ernesto Sabato") graduated in physics at this university; he went on to teach at the [Sorbonne](/wiki/University_of_Paris "University of Paris") and the [MIT](/wiki/Massachusetts_Institute_of_Technology "Massachusetts Institute of Technology") before becoming a famed novelist. Doctor [René Favaloro](/wiki/Ren%C3%A9_Favaloro "René Favaloro") was another famous alumnus. During its early years, the university attracted a number of intellectuals from the Spanish-speaking world, such as Dominican [Pedro Henríquez Ureña](/wiki/Pedro_Henr%C3%ADquez_Ure%C3%B1a "Pedro Henríquez Ureña").

[San Ponciano church](/w/index.php?title=San_Ponciano_church&action=edit&redlink=1 "San Ponciano church (page does not exist)") [[es](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bas%C3%ADlica_de_San_Ponciano_(La_Plata) "es:Basílica de San Ponciano (La Plata)")] is on the corner of 48th and 5th Streets. It was the first chapel in La Plata and Pedro Benoit himself drew up the plans for the church. It was inaugurated on 19 November 1883, the first anniversary of the foundation of the city. Its [neo-Gothic style](/wiki/Gothic_Revival_architecture "Gothic Revival architecture") has been well kept, and the inner paintings are now being restored. Then-governor Rocha was the one to name it "San Ponciano". This was both in memory of his son Ponciano and in honor of [Pope Pontian](/wiki/Pope_Pontian "Pope Pontian"). Inside the church is the "Virgen de Luján" niche, which was moved here in 1904.

### La Plata in the 20th century

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: La Plata in the 20th century")]

Under [Alvear](/wiki/Marcelo_Torcuato_de_Alvear "Marcelo Torcuato de Alvear")'s administration (1922–1928), [Enrique Mosconi](/wiki/Enrique_Mosconi "Enrique Mosconi"), the president of the state oil company *[Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales](/wiki/Yacimientos_Petrol%C3%ADferos_Fiscales "Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales")*, created the La Plata [distillery](/wiki/Distillery "Distillery"), at the time the tenth largest in the world.[[5]](#cite_note-Pigna153-5)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e6/La_Plata_-_Estaci%C3%B3n_del_Ferrocarril_Roca.jpg/250px-La_Plata_-_Estaci%C3%B3n_del_Ferrocarril_Roca.jpg)](/wiki/File:La_Plata_-_Estaci%C3%B3n_del_Ferrocarril_Roca.jpg)

Railway station, La Plata.

On 10 December 1945, in the Parish church of St. Francis of Assisi in this city, [Juan Domingo Perón](/wiki/Juan_Domingo_Per%C3%B3n "Juan Domingo Perón") and [Eva Duarte](/wiki/Eva_Duarte "Eva Duarte") were married.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In 1952, the city was renamed [Ciudad Eva Perón](/wiki/Ciudad_Eva_Per%C3%B3n "Ciudad Eva Perón"); its original name was restored in 1955.

In March 1976, the Argentine military seized power following a coup d'état, which involved the disappearance of a number of students from La Plata. The military junta had implemented what was called the [National Reorganization Process](/wiki/National_Reorganization_Process "National Reorganization Process") which was a set of policies used by the regime to destroy left-wing guerrilla forces and oppress resistance to its rule. The process included kidnappings, torture and murder. Meanwhile, the [Montoneros](/wiki/Montoneros "Montoneros"), a leftist guerilla group, responded violently to the junta and its actions as they enlisted other Argentines to join their campaign against the regime. Those enlisted included young, left-wing, politically active students from the organization named the *Unión de Estudiantes Secundarios* (Union of High School Students) of La Plata. The UES was committed to achieving school reforms and other political reforms through demonstrations and protests that irked the ruling regime. Many of these students were kidnapped and killed (many remain as '[desaparecidos](/wiki/Enforced_disappearance "Enforced disappearance")') as part of the [state's terrorism](/wiki/State_terrorism "State terrorism") during the [dictatorship](/wiki/Dirty_War "Dirty War").

In October 1998, [UNESCO](/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO") approved the city's bid to gain recognition as a [World Heritage](/wiki/World_Heritage "World Heritage") site. The approval is still pending due to various objections to the criterion of maintaining architectural and landscape features during recent decades, which in the opinion of other specialists, has caused severe damage to the original design and contextual aesthetics.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

### Sports and stadiums

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Sports and stadiums")]

The city is home to two important [first division](/wiki/Primera_Divisi%C3%B3n_Argentina "Primera División Argentina") [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") teams: [Estudiantes de La Plata](/wiki/Estudiantes_de_La_Plata "Estudiantes de La Plata") and [Gimnasia y Esgrima de La Plata](/wiki/Gimnasia_y_Esgrima_La_Plata "Gimnasia y Esgrima La Plata").

Estudiantes de La Plata is the most successful club in the city, having won seven national tournaments, four [Copa Libertadores](/wiki/Copa_Libertadores "Copa Libertadores") and the [Intercontinental Cup](/wiki/Intercontinental_Cup_(1960%E2%80%932004) "Intercontinental Cup (1960–2004)") against the [Manchester United](/wiki/Manchester_United_F.C. "Manchester United F.C.") in 1968. It is the origin of several symbols of Argentine football such as [Osvaldo Zubeldía](/wiki/Osvaldo_Zubeld%C3%ADa "Osvaldo Zubeldía"), [Carlos Bilardo](/wiki/Carlos_Bilardo "Carlos Bilardo"), [Alejandro Sabella](/wiki/Alejandro_Sabella "Alejandro Sabella"), [Juan Ramón](/wiki/Juan_Ram%C3%B3n_Ver%C3%B3n "Juan Ramón Verón") and [Juan Sebastián Verón](/wiki/Juan_Sebasti%C3%A1n_Ver%C3%B3n "Juan Sebastián Verón"). Estudiantes has had a great influence on the [Argentine National Team](/wiki/Argentina_national_football_team "Argentina national football team"), mainly through Bilardo in the [1986 World Cup](/wiki/1986_FIFA_World_Cup "1986 FIFA World Cup"). Its successes and its style of play, normally called [bilardismo](/wiki/Carlos_Bilardo "Carlos Bilardo"), have placed it in the position of exponent of a football style deeply rooted in the country.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

For its part, Gimnasia de La Plata, founded in 1887, has not obtained titles in the professional era, but it is a traditional First Division club.

The [Estadio Ciudad de La Plata](/wiki/Estadio_Ciudad_de_La_Plata "Estadio Ciudad de La Plata"), also known as the "Estadio Único", opened on 7 June 2003, as one of the most modern football stadiums in Latin America. Various other construction and renovation projects have continued, including the addition of a roof structure. [Estudiantes](/wiki/Estudiantes_de_La_Plata "Estudiantes de La Plata") played in the new stadium from 2006 to 2019 while their [own stadium](/wiki/Estadio_Jorge_Luis_Hirschi "Estadio Jorge Luis Hirschi") was being modernized; Estudiantes returned to its traditional home ground of [Jorge Luis Hirschi Stadium](/wiki/Jorge_Luis_Hirschi_Stadium "Jorge Luis Hirschi Stadium") in 2019.[[12]](#cite_note-12) [Gimnasia La Plata](/wiki/Club_de_Gimnasia_y_Esgrima_La_Plata "Club de Gimnasia y Esgrima La Plata") has only played occasional home games in the Estadio Único. In 2011, the Estadio Ciudad de La Plata was one of the host sites for the [2011 Copa America](/wiki/2011_Copa_America "2011 Copa America") including an opening-round match between [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina_national_football_team "Argentina national football team") and [Bolivia](/wiki/Bolivia_national_football_team "Bolivia national football team"), a semi-final game, and the third-place final. Now, the stadium is additionally used for concerts and [Puma](/wiki/Argentina_national_rugby_union_team "Argentina national rugby union team") matches.

[Jorge Luis Hirschi Stadium](/wiki/Jorge_Luis_Hirschi_Stadium "Jorge Luis Hirschi Stadium"), the home ground of Estudiantes, is located on 1st Avenue in La Plata. [Estadio Juan Carmelo Zerillo](/wiki/Estadio_Juan_Carmelo_Zerillo "Estadio Juan Carmelo Zerillo"), the home ground of Gimnasia La Plata, is located in a park known as El Bosque.

During 2009, following a series of agreements between the city municipality, the governor of the province and the nation's presidency, progress was made in the final transfer of the land of the Paseo del Bosque to [Estudiantes](/wiki/Estudiantes_de_La_Plata "Estudiantes de La Plata") and [Gimnasia La Plata](/wiki/Club_de_Gimnasia_y_Esgrima_La_Plata "Club de Gimnasia y Esgrima La Plata") clubs. On 24 June 2009, the Deliberative Council adopted the convention and the ordinance for which Gimnasia and Estudiantes clubs received "grants" for the lands on which their home grounds are currently located within El Bosque (The Forest).

### Elections and civic advances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Elections and civic advances")]

On 28 October 2007, [Pablo Bruera](/wiki/Pablo_Bruera "Pablo Bruera") was elected mayor with 26% of the votes, replacing [Julio Alak](/wiki/Julio_Alak "Julio Alak"), who had been mayor since 1991.

On 25 February 2009, La Plata debuted a parking system that uses text messaging (SMS), thus becoming the first city in Argentina to control parking using technology applications.

The Pasaje Rodrigo, a traditional "galería" (the older version of shopping malls in Argentina), reopened its doors in April 2009 as Pasaje Rodrigo shopping mall, after having been closed to the public for 10 years. It had originally been opened in 1929 by Spanish immigrant Basilio Rodrigo.

On 25 October 2015, Julio Garro was elected mayor with 41.35% of the votes, replacing [Pablo Bruera](/wiki/Pablo_Bruera "Pablo Bruera"), who had been mayor since 2007. Garro was reelected for a second term in 2019.[[13]](#cite_note-13) In the [2023 local elections](/wiki/2023_Buenos_Aires_provincial_election "2023 Buenos Aires provincial election"), former mayor Alak defeated Garro in an historically close election.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Geography")]

### Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Location")]

Located in the north-eastern area of the [province of Buenos Aires](/wiki/Province_of_Buenos_Aires "Province of Buenos Aires"), La Plata is surrounded by [Ensenada](/wiki/Ensenada_Partido "Ensenada Partido") and [Berisso](/wiki/Berisso_Partido "Berisso Partido") to the northeast, [Berazategui](/wiki/Berazategui_Partido "Berazategui Partido") and [Florencio Varela](/wiki/Florencio_Varela_Partido "Florencio Varela Partido") to the northwest, [San Vicente](/wiki/San_Vicente_Partido "San Vicente Partido") and [Coronel Brandsen](/wiki/Brandsen_Partido "Brandsen Partido") to the southwest and south, and [Magdalena](/wiki/Magdalena_Partido "Magdalena Partido"), to the southeast, occupying an area of 893 km2.

The metropolitan area of La Plata includes the neighborhoods of Tolosa, Ringuelet, Manuel B. Gonnet, City Bell, Villa Elisa, Melchor Romero, Abasto, Gorina, José Hernández, Ángel Etcheverry, Arturo Seguí, Los Hornos, Lisandro Olmos, Villa Elvira and Altos de San Lorenzo, all of which have community centers that operate as local delegations.

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Climate")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/30/Clima_La_Plata_%28Argentina%29.PNG/250px-Clima_La_Plata_%28Argentina%29.PNG)](/wiki/File:Clima_La_Plata_(Argentina).PNG)

Climogram

La Plata has a [humid subtropical climate](/wiki/Humid_subtropical_climate "Humid subtropical climate") (*Cfa* under the [Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification")).[[15]](#cite_note-koppen-15) During winter, temperatures are cool during the day and cold during the night, even reaching below freezing.[[16]](#cite_note-SMN-16) The average temperature in the coldest month, July, is 8.9 °C (48.0 °F). Winters tend to be cloudier than summer, averaging around 10 overcast days from June to August, compared to 6 overcast days from December to February.[[16]](#cite_note-SMN-16) Summers are warm to hot with a January high of 29 °C (84.2 °F) while nighttime temperatures are cooler, averaging 18 °C (64.4 °F). Spring and fall are transition seasons, featuring warm daytime temperatures and cool nighttime temperatures, highly variable with some days reaching above 32 °C (89.6 °F) and below 0 °C (32.0 °F).[[16]](#cite_note-SMN-16) The city is fairly humid, owing to its coastal location, having an average monthly humidity higher than 75%. La Plata receives 1,092 millimetres (43 in) of precipitation annually, with winters being the drier months and summer the wetter months. On average, La Plata receives 2,285 hours of sunshine a year, or 51% of possible sunshine, ranging from a low of 41% in June and July to 62% in February.[[17]](#cite_note-NOAA-17) The highest temperature recorded was 41 °C (106 °F) on 14 January 2022 while the lowest temperature recorded was −5.7 °C (22 °F) on 14 June 1967.[[18]](#cite_note-SMNextremes-18)

Snowfall is extremely rare in the city with only 5 major snowfall events: July 1912, 1928, 22 June 1981, 9 July 2007 during the [July 2007 Argentine winter storm](/wiki/July_2007_Argentine_winter_storm "July 2007 Argentine winter storm"),[[19]](#cite_note-19) and on 6 June 2012.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)

#### Earthquakes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Earthquakes")]

The region lies on the Punta del Este fault, and its latest event occurred on 30 November 2018 at 10:27 UTC−3 with a magnitude of 3.8 on the Richter scale.[[24]](#cite_note-24) This earthquake was extremely unusual in La Plata, a city where the last earthquake had been on 5 June 1888 (128 years before) with a magnitude of 5.5 on the Richter scale.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Government")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Casa_de_Gobierno_de_la_Provincia_de_Buenos_Aires-La_Plata-1.jpg/250px-Casa_de_Gobierno_de_la_Provincia_de_Buenos_Aires-La_Plata-1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Casa_de_Gobierno_de_la_Provincia_de_Buenos_Aires-La_Plata-1.jpg)

Casa de Gobierno de la Provincia de Buenos Aires.

Given the federal system of [government in Argentina](/wiki/Government_of_Argentina "Government of Argentina") there are three orders or ranks: the National, Provincial and Municipal.

Power of the executive branch in La Plata is exercised by the municipal mayor, elected by popular vote every four years with the possibility of unlimited reelection. The town hall is known as the Palacio Municipal, and is located in the block surrounded by streets 51, 53, 11 and 12, in the city center. It is one of the important buildings that surround Plaza Moreno, and is opposite the cathedral.

The city government is divided into different areas. These are: Private Secretary, Secretary General, Ministry of Economy, Ministry of Public Management, Social Development Secretariat, Ministry of Culture, Ministry of Modernization and Economic Development, Ministry of Justice, Secretary of Health and Social Medicine, Chief of Staff, Regional Market La Plata, Management Consortium Puerto La Plata, Executive Unit Revenue Agency, Environment Agency Implementation Unit, Human Rights Department, Ministry of Government, Policy Planning Council, Regional Production and Employment, and the city council.

As it is the capital of the Province of Buenos Aires, La Plata is also home to the three provincial powers: the provincial executive (led at the moment by the [Governor](/wiki/Governor_of_the_Buenos_Aires_Province "Governor of the Buenos Aires Province")) along with its ministries, the judiciary, and the [provincial Legislature](/wiki/Legislature_of_Buenos_Aires_Province "Legislature of Buenos Aires Province").

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Economy")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/Banco_Provincia_Casa_Matriz-La_Plata-1.jpg/250px-Banco_Provincia_Casa_Matriz-La_Plata-1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Banco_Provincia_Casa_Matriz-La_Plata-1.jpg)

[Banco Provincia](/wiki/Bank_of_the_Province_of_Buenos_Aires "Bank of the Province of Buenos Aires") headquarters in La Plata.

According to the National Economic Survey 2004–2005, out of a total of 23,844 local listings 90% are dedicated to the production of goods and services, 4% belonged to the Civil Service, 2% are for semi sideshows or removable; 1% are for worship, political parties and unions, and the remaining 2% was in the process of classification.

### Finance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Finance")]

La Plata has a stock market, the Bolsa de Comercio, which was founded in 1960 and is a member of the Argentine stock market system.

In the city there are branches of major banks operating in the country, including [Banco Nación](/wiki/Banco_Naci%C3%B3n "Banco Nación"), Banco Provincia, Banco Ciudad de Buenos Aires, [Banco Hipotecario](/wiki/Banco_Hipotecario "Banco Hipotecario"), [HSBC](/wiki/HSBC "HSBC"), [Citigroup](/wiki/Citigroup "Citigroup"), [Banco Itaú](/wiki/Banco_Ita%C3%BA "Banco Itaú"), Francés, Macro, [Standard Bank](/wiki/Standard_Bank "Standard Bank"), etc.

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Transport")]

*[Trenes Argentinos](/wiki/Operadora_Ferroviaria_Sociedad_del_Estado "Operadora Ferroviaria Sociedad del Estado")* operate a rail service ([Roca Line](/wiki/Roca_Line "Roca Line")) between [La Plata station](/wiki/La_Plata_station_(Argentina) "La Plata station (Argentina)") and [Constitución station](/wiki/Constituci%C3%B3n_railway_station "Constitución railway station") in Buenos Aires via Berazategui. It runs every 24 to 30 minutes Mondays to Saturdays and every 40 minutes on Sundays.[[26]](#cite_note-26) In addition, there is a shuttle service *[Tren Universitario](/wiki/University_train_of_La_Plata "University train of La Plata")* between La Plata station and Policlínico operating five to eight times a day.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

The city's general aviation needs are served by [La Plata Airport](/wiki/La_Plata_Airport "La Plata Airport"), although it has no commercial service as of 2023. The nearest commercially served airport is [Ministro Pistarini International Airport](/wiki/Ministro_Pistarini_International_Airport "Ministro Pistarini International Airport") in Buenos Aires.

### International communities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: International communities")]

The Spanish community's contributions include the renowned Spanish Hospital. Spain has also acknowledged the community by installing a consulate in the city of La Plata.

The Arab community also has several institutions, including the [Syriac Orthodox](/wiki/Syriac_Orthodox "Syriac Orthodox") Welfare Assoc, Assoc Islamic Argentino de La Plata, and the Lebanese Society of La Plata.

The Jewish community of La Plata has numerous institutions. These include AMIA La Plata[[33]](#cite_note-33) (Asociación Mutual Israelita Argentina), which has under its orbit Hebrew School Chaim Nachman Bialik and the Jewish cemetery of the city, the Beit Chabad La Plata,[[34]](#cite_note-34) part of [Chabad Lubavitch Argentina](/wiki/Chabad "Chabad").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1e/Juzgados_Federales-La_Plata-1.jpg/250px-Juzgados_Federales-La_Plata-1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Juzgados_Federales-La_Plata-1.jpg)

Juzgados Federales de La Plata (ex Hotel Provincial).

La Plata is a [planned city](/wiki/New_town "New town"), characterized by a strict grid of square blocks, with diagonal avenues running across. The two major diagonals, 73 and 74, go across the city from east to west and from north to south, respectively, and converge right in the city's center at Plaza Moreno. This square houses the so-called "foundation stone" and is the main square of the city. To its sides are located the [City Hall](/wiki/La_Plata_City_Hall "La Plata City Hall") and the [cathedral of La Plata](/wiki/Cathedral_of_La_Plata "Cathedral of La Plata"). Highlights of the city are the Museum of Natural Sciences, the building of the Interior, the Provincial Legislature, the new theater and the hippodrome. Many of these buildings were built at the time of the founding of the city, following an international call for proposals.

Another work is the parent company of *Banco de la Provincia de Buenos Aires*, located between the streets 6, 7, 46 and 47. This building was designed by architects Juan A. Buschiazzo and Luis Viglione. It opened on 19 April 1886, being amended in 1913 and in the 1970s.

It is a very green city, with its largest park being the "*Paseo del Bosque*" (or simply 'the forest'). Twenty-two other parks and squares contain much of the city's trees and greenery. Other landmarks include the lake, the Martin Fierro amphitheater, the Parque Pereyra Iraola, the Zoo and Botanical Garden, the Victorian astronomical observatory, and the Natural History Museum. One notable attraction is the Children's Republic, which is said to have inspired [Walt Disney](/wiki/Walt_Disney "Walt Disney") to build [Disneyland](/wiki/Disneyland "Disneyland") after he visited it during a trip to Argentina.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 1977 the city lost one of its most valuable monuments to a fire: the [Teatro Argentino de La Plata](/wiki/Teatro_Argentino_de_La_Plata "Teatro Argentino de La Plata"), predominantly built in a neoclassic style. After the fire, a new [brutalist style](/wiki/Brutalist_style "Brutalist style") theater was built in its place. The [Estadio Ciudad de La Plata](/wiki/Estadio_Ciudad_de_La_Plata "Estadio Ciudad de La Plata"), a stadium influenced by modern high-tech architecture, is located in the city. It was planned and built after a national competition during the 1970s. The stadium received repairs and amendments in a subsequent project to expand its capacity, and also to rebuild the roof in sectors that had been destroyed by storms.[[40]](#cite_note-40) The construction of a semitransparent deck of [Kevlar](/wiki/Kevlar "Kevlar") and plastic resins was finished in January 2011.[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*]

La Plata has one commercial airport, [Aeropuerto de La Plata](/wiki/La_Plata_Airport "La Plata Airport"), which is not served by any airlines.

### Awards received by the city

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Awards received by the city")]

The city was awarded at the [Exposition Universelle](/wiki/Exposition_Universelle_(1889) "Exposition Universelle (1889)") in Paris in 1889, an event in which the new city was given the two gold medals awarded in the categories "City of the Future" and "Better performance built." At that event, Jules Verne gave the award to Dardo Rocha[[41]](#cite_note-41)

### La Plata Cemetery

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: La Plata Cemetery")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/Jacaranda_%281%29.jpg/250px-Jacaranda_%281%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Jacaranda_(1).jpg)

[Jacaranda](/wiki/Jacaranda "Jacaranda") foliage and blossom

The municipal [cemetery of La Plata](/w/index.php?title=Cemetery_of_La_Plata&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cemetery of La Plata (page does not exist)") was established in 1886 for the new capital of the province of Buenos Aires. It was designed by [Pedro Benoit](/wiki/Pedro_Benoit "Pedro Benoit"). It lies on the intersection of Avenue 31, 72 and diagonal 74 in the southern tip. It has some remarkable architectural features, both in its main entrance and in many of the family vaults, including [neoclassical](/wiki/Neoclassical_architecture "Neoclassical architecture"), [Neo-Gothic](/wiki/Gothic_Revival_architecture "Gothic Revival architecture"), [Art Nouveau](/wiki/Modern_architecture "Modern architecture") (in its variant of [Catalan Art Nouveau](/wiki/Modernisme "Modernisme")), [Art Deco](/wiki/Art_Deco "Art Deco") and [Egyptian revival](/wiki/Egyptian_Revival_architecture "Egyptian Revival architecture") styles. The main entrance is an impressive neo-classical portico with [Doric columns](/wiki/Doric_order "Doric order"). The Catholic chapel, in Romanesque revival style, was finished in 1950. Diagonal 74 begins at the Río de la Plata and ends at the cemetery, causing some to remark that the layout may have been intentional symbolism of the cycle of life and death by Benoit.[[42]](#cite_note-42)

Its annex, the Jewish Cemetery, belongs to the Asociación Mutual Israelita Argentina in La Plata and is located on Avenue 72.

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Sports")]

The main [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") teams in the city are [Estudiantes de La Plata](/wiki/Club_Estudiantes_de_La_Plata "Club Estudiantes de La Plata") (locally known as "pinchas"), winners of the [Intercontinental Cup](/wiki/Intercontinental_Cup_(1960%E2%80%932004) "Intercontinental Cup (1960–2004)") in 1968, and [Gimnasia y Esgrima La Plata](/wiki/Club_de_Gimnasia_y_Esgrima_La_Plata "Club de Gimnasia y Esgrima La Plata"), both of which currently play in the Argentina's [first division](/wiki/Primera_Divisi%C3%B3n_Argentina "Primera División Argentina"). Estudiantes is the sole team within the city to win a national or international title. La Plata has [Liga Amateur Platense de fútbol](/w/index.php?title=Liga_Amateur_Platense_de_f%C3%BAtbol&action=edit&redlink=1 "Liga Amateur Platense de fútbol (page does not exist)") that encompasses various clubs in the region, such as [Club Atlético Estrella de Berisso](/w/index.php?title=Club_Atl%C3%A9tico_Estrella_de_Berisso&action=edit&redlink=1 "Club Atlético Estrella de Berisso (page does not exist)"), [La Plata FC](/wiki/La_Plata_FC "La Plata FC"), [Asociación Nueva Alianza](/w/index.php?title=Asociaci%C3%B3n_Nueva_Alianza&action=edit&redlink=1 "Asociación Nueva Alianza (page does not exist)"), and [Club Everton](/w/index.php?title=Club_Atl%C3%A9tico_Everton&action=edit&redlink=1 "Club Atlético Everton (page does not exist)").

Basketball has a place in the [Torneo Nacional de Ascenso](/wiki/Torneo_Nacional_de_Ascenso "Torneo Nacional de Ascenso") (National Ascent Tournament), through [Gimnasia y Esgrima La Plata](/wiki/Club_de_Gimnasia_y_Esgrima_La_Plata "Club de Gimnasia y Esgrima La Plata"). In addition, through the Asociación Platense de Basquetbol, La Plata has leagues and tournaments for all levels and categories, including Gimnasia y Esgrima La Plata, Estudiantes, Centro Fomento de Los Hornos Club Atenas, [Unión Vecinal](/w/index.php?title=Uni%C3%B3n_Vecinal&action=edit&redlink=1 "Unión Vecinal (page does not exist)"), [Centro de Fomento Meridiano V](/w/index.php?title=Centro_de_Fomento_Meridiano_V&action=edit&redlink=1 "Centro de Fomento Meridiano V (page does not exist)"), and [Club Cultural y Deportivo Juventud](/w/index.php?title=Club_Cultural_y_Deportivo_Juventud&action=edit&redlink=1 "Club Cultural y Deportivo Juventud (page does not exist)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f6/Aut%C3%B3dromo_Roberto_Jos%C3%A9_Mouras.svg/250px-Aut%C3%B3dromo_Roberto_Jos%C3%A9_Mouras.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Aut%C3%B3dromo_Roberto_Jos%C3%A9_Mouras.svg)

Map of the [Autódromo Roberto José Mouras](/wiki/Aut%C3%B3dromo_Roberto_Jos%C3%A9_Mouras "Autódromo Roberto José Mouras") track

[Auto racing](/wiki/Auto_racing "Auto racing") also has its importance in the city in the form of the [Turismo Carretera](/wiki/Turismo_Carretera "Turismo Carretera"). There is also a race track named [Autódromo Roberto José Mouras](/wiki/Aut%C3%B3dromo_Roberto_Jos%C3%A9_Mouras "Autódromo Roberto José Mouras") in honor of the historic [Chevrolet](/wiki/Chevrolet "Chevrolet") driver died in [Lobos](/wiki/Lobos "Lobos") in 1992.[[43]](#cite_note-43) [Gastón Mazzacane](/wiki/Gast%C3%B3n_Mazzacane "Gastón Mazzacane") is from the city as well. After 21 races in [Formula 1](/wiki/Formula_One "Formula One"), he also competed in the [Champ Car](/wiki/Champ_Car "Champ Car") and [Top Race V6](/wiki/Top_Race_V6 "Top Race V6") series.

The [1970 Artistic Billiards World Championship](/wiki/Artistic_Billiards_World_Championship "Artistic Billiards World Championship") were held in La Plata.

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Culture")]

Culture has a major role in the city of La Plata. This is reflected in the large amount of cultural centers, theaters, museums, cinemas and libraries that are in the city, as well as the [Universidad Nacional de La Plata](/wiki/National_University_of_La_Plata "National University of La Plata") and the [observatory](/wiki/La_Plata_Astronomical_Observatory "La Plata Astronomical Observatory").

### Festivals, celebrations and events scheduled

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Festivals, celebrations and events scheduled")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/ba/Quemamu%C3%B1eco2008.JPG/250px-Quemamu%C3%B1eco2008.JPG)](/wiki/File:Quemamu%C3%B1eco2008.JPG)

Doll winner 2008, "Arde Troya".

The city of La Plata has the particularity of being the only place (with [Berisso](/wiki/Berisso "Berisso") and [Ensenada](/wiki/Ensenada,_Buenos_Aires "Ensenada, Buenos Aires")) in the country where the burning of Momos (Dolls similar to those cremated at the [Fallas festival](/wiki/Falls_Festival "Falls Festival") in [Valencia](/wiki/Valencia "Valencia"), Spain) is held every New Year's Eve. Hundreds of dolls are burnt to celebrate the end of the year and the beginning of a new year. Competitions for the best doll is awarded by the La Plata municipality and media.[[46]](#cite_note-46)[[47]](#cite_note-47)

Every 18 April is [International Day For Monuments and Sites](/wiki/International_Day_For_Monuments_and_Sites "International Day For Monuments and Sites").[[48]](#cite_note-48) That day, buses with tour guides leave from the Centro Cultural Pasaje Dardo Rocha to the various historical monuments of La Plata, Berisso and Ensenada, being the activity free.

In addition, every year on 19 November, the anniversary of the city is held with recitals and a fireworks show at the Plaza Moreno.

In La Plata, as well as in all of Argentina, the first day of spring is celebrated along with [National Student's Day](/wiki/Student%27s_Day_(Argentina) "Student's Day (Argentina)") on 21 September. On this date, the municipality organizes concerts both in the Paseo del Bosque and Plaza Moreno.

### Patron saint

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Patron saint")]

The patron saint of the city and the party of La Plata is Saint Ponciano, the 18th pope of the Catholic Church, who died in [Sardinia](/wiki/Sardinia "Sardinia") on 19 November 235.[[49]](#cite_note-49) On 19 November is the feast of the Catholic parish of [St. Pontian](/wiki/Pope_Pontian "Pope Pontian"), pope and martyr.[[50]](#cite_note-50)

## Tourism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Tourism")]

The most important tourist sites are located in the heart of the city's founding, between Streets 51 and 53, being the center of the city's Plaza Moreno. The square separates two great works of the city: the [Metropolitan Cathedral of La Plata "Immaculate Conception"](/wiki/Cathedral_of_La_Plata "Cathedral of La Plata") and the [Palacio Municipal](/wiki/La_Plata_City_Hall "La Plata City Hall").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Casa_Curutchet_y_edificio_lindero.jpg/250px-Casa_Curutchet_y_edificio_lindero.jpg)](/wiki/File:Casa_Curutchet_y_edificio_lindero.jpg)

[Curutchet House](/wiki/Curutchet_House "Curutchet House"), [World Heritage Site](/wiki/List_of_World_Heritage_Sites_in_Argentina "List of World Heritage Sites in Argentina") in La Plata

The city of La Plata has many monuments and historical sites. Among them are: the Banco Provincia de Buenos Aires headquarters, the [Curutchet House](/wiki/Curutchet_House "Curutchet House"), Casa de Gobierno Provincial, Casa Mariani – Teruggi, Centro Cultural Islas Malvinas, Centro Cultural Meridiano V, Centro Cultural Pasaje Dardo Rocha, Iglesia San Benjamín, la Legislatura Provincial, Museo de Ciencias Naturales, the Quinta Oreste Santospago, the Rectorado de la UNLP, Museo Ferroviario of Tolosa, the Teatro Municipal Coliseo Podestá, the Anfiteatro Martín Fierro, the [Estadio Ciudad de La Plata](/wiki/Estadio_Ciudad_de_La_Plata "Estadio Ciudad de La Plata"), entre otros.

Moreover, staying at the city of La Plata the visitors can tour the monuments of the neighboring cities of Berisso and Ensenada (formerly part of La Plata), finding among these to the Street New York, the Swift Refrigerator, [Ukrainian Catholic](/wiki/Ukrainian_Catholic "Ukrainian Catholic") Parish Our Lady of the Assumption, Old Station Cultural Centre, Fort Barragán and Historical Museum, the Ensenada Rotary Bridge, among others.

*Quema de muñecos* is a traditional celebration held every New Year.[[51]](#cite_note-51) People from different parts of the city build giant figures, usually modeled after famous fictional characters, using wood and [papier mâché](/wiki/Papier_m%C3%A2ch%C3%A9 "Papier mâché"), and stuff them with fireworks. After midnight, all the figures are burnt, igniting the firework-filling in the process, resulting in a fiery spectacle. For safety reasons, most figures are built in the same spot where they are planned to be burnt, which usually involves a large clearing in a plaza, wide [median strips](/wiki/Median_strip "Median strip"), or abandoned lots. After the celebration, residents vote for the best figure in terms of design, size, etc., and the most voted gets a prize from a panel of established figure builders, as well as cash money reward.

## Notable companies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_Plata&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Notable companies")]

Among the companies with headquarters, origins, or significant operations in La Plata are:

* [Banco de la Provincia de Buenos Aires](/wiki/Bank_of_the_Province_of_Buenos_Aires "Bank of the Province of Buenos Aires"), the provincial public bank, whose Casa Matriz in La Plata was inaugurated in 1886.[[52]](#cite_note-52)
* Federación Patronal Seguros, an insurance group founded in La Plata in 1923 by local businesspeople.[[53]](#cite_note-53)
* Rivadavia Seguros, an insurance company whose Casa Central is located in La Plata.[[54]](#cite_note-54)
* ABSA (Aguas Bonaerenses S.A.), a water and sanitation operator whose service area covers numerous localities in Buenos Aires Province.[[55]](#cite_note-55)
* EDELAP, the electricity distributor for La Plata and surrounding districts such as Berisso, Ensenada, Brandsen and Punta Indio.[[56]](#cite_note-56)
* [El Día](/wiki/El_D%C3%ADa_(La_Plata) "El Día (La Plata)"), a daily newspaper founded in 1884 and published in La Plata.[[57]](#cite_note-57)
* *Diario Hoy*, a newspaper launched in La Plata in 1993.[[58]](#cite_note-58)
* [Makenhaus](/wiki/Makenhaus "Makenhaus"), a real-estate developer focused on residential projects in La Plata, [City Bell](/wiki/City_Bell "City Bell"), [Manuel B. Gonnet](/wiki/Manuel_B._Gonnet "Manuel B. Gonnet"), [Buenos Aires Province](/wiki/Buenos_Aires_Province "Buenos Aires Province"), [Buenos Aires](/wiki/Buenos_Aires "Buenos Aires"), and [Florida](/wiki/Florida "Florida"), [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").[[59]](#cite_note-refMakenhausSite-59)[[60]](#cite_note-refExpansion2024-60)
* Red 92, a radio and digital news outlet based in La Plata.[[61]](#cite_note-61)
* Nini Mayorista, a wholesale retailer that began in La Plata in 1969 and later expanded its operations.[[62]](#cite_note-62)
* Molino Campodónico, a flour-milling company whose history dates back to 1888.[[63]](#cite_note-63)
* Cerámica Ctibor, a construction-materials manufacturer with a long industrial presence in the La Plata region.[[64]](#cite_note-64)
* Conteplat, a packaging manufacturer based in the La Plata industrial park and dedicated to PET preforms.[[65]](#cite_note-65)[[66]](#cite_note-66)
* Snoop Consulting, a software company that opened a development center in La Plata and is part of the local technology cluster.[[67]](#cite_note-67)[[68]](#cite_note-68)
* Gisens Biotech, a biotechnology startup from La Plata linked to research carried out with participation from the [National University of La Plata](/wiki/National_University_of_La_Plata "National University of La Plata") and [CONICET](/wiki/CONICET "CONICET").[[69]](#cite_note-69)[[70]](#cite_note-70)
